name: Open ground construction; on ground
description:
This cradle-to-gate unit process dataset is for the production of Open ground construction; on ground.
This unit process includes raw material acquisition; manufacturing; transport; production and use of electricity; disposal of process waste; ancillary materials manufacturing; and transport of service personnel.

processDocumentation.timeDescription: The period spans from the publication date of the latest study used in Jungbluth et al. (2012) to the publication date of Frischknecht et al. (2020), since the model has remained effectively the same throughout this period.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: Assumed, since a large fraction of the data used for the inventory in Jungbluth et al. (2012) comes from Swiss publications.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription:
This process represent the construction of an open ground installation for residential photovoltaic systems for residential photovoltaic systems using average technology for Europe from the calendar years 1991-2008.
Open ground starts by positioning foundation profiles, which are then piled or screwed into the ground and the height leveled. Finally, the rest of the system is mounted and the panels fixed.
processDocumentation.modelingConstantsDescription: Jungbluth et al. (2012) assumed a life time of 30 years, which is reflected in the occupation exchanges. A standard distance of 60 km is assumed for truck transport, 200 km and 600 km for train transport (to which materials does each distance applied is not specified), and 100 km for the van transport.
processDocumentation.completenessDescription:
Treatment of Missing Environmental Data
Jungbluth et al. (2012) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.
Treatment of Missing Technosphere Data
Jungbluth et al. (2012) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.
Mass Balance
No mass balance was calculated for this process.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: This unit process was modeled based on Frischknecht et al. (2020), which in turn is based on Jungbluth et al. (2012) inventory for an open ground system available on Table 11.18 of said report.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription:
The values in the LCI in Frischknecht et al. (2020) and Jungbluth et al. (2012) reflect the expert judgement of the authors, since they do not seem a weighted average of the different studies.
Process data quality
Based on the information included in the “Data completeness section”, the data quality characteristic (DQC) "Process completeness" was given a score of 2 (60-79 % of determined flow have been evaluated and given a value). It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.
processDocumentation.samplingDescription:
System boundaries
The system boundary is cradle to gate and includes material use, waste treatment, and transport. 
Data collection
The data that made Jungbluth et al. (2012) LCI is based on previous reports. See the original source for details.
Uncertainty
Frischknecht et al. (2020) included a standard deviation for all flows, which is repeated here.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: Between calendar years 1991 and 2006, both included, based on the studies cited in Jungbluth et al. (2012)

reviews.details: Colleagues at ESU-services Ldt. are acknowledged for their revision of data, but no other details on their review are available. Because it is not properly documented, the "Process review" data quality entry is left as "5: Not documented".

Colleagues at the Paul Scherrer Institute are acknowledged for their revision of data, but no other details on their review are available.  Because it is not properly documented, the "Process review" data quality entry is left as "5: Not documented"

processDocumentation.intendedApplication:
Situation C2 - Accounting, without system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system excluding interactions with other systems in the LCI model.

The target audience of this model are LCA practitioners, the concrete and related industries, and the general public. The inventory is considered largely complete, except for land transformation activities. For that reason, it is not recommended to use this dataset when evaluating land use impact categories. The results of the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s data gap analysis were analyzed using the ISO 21930-LCIA-US impact assessment methodology. 

The original application of this dataset was the identification of data gaps in the Federal LCA Commons (FLCAC) (see "Project" above). However, the intended application is to provide a cradle-to-gate inventory (A1-A3, according to ISO 21930) using exclusively processes currently available in USLCI or generated as part of the project mentioned above, and elementary flows from the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) to enable the use of a fuller range of LCIA impact categories than those reported in EPDs. For data quality purposes, the intended application is to provide a US average inventory for the year of publication of the original source.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed as part of the "Public Life Cycle Inventory Data Gap Analysis through Process Modeling" National Institute of Standards and Technology  Technical Note 2338. This dataset was developed after the publication of the Technical Note, and thus it is not discussed there.
